title: [Bug]: Metrics snapshots can emit non-standard JSON numbers
summary: Metrics snapshot creation and writing can accept non-finite float values. Python JSON output may then emit NaN or Infinity, which is not valid interoperable JSON for local operators, scripts, or observability connectors.
observed: A gauge or observation value of NaN can reach metrics_snapshot() and write_metrics_snapshot(), producing a non-standard JSON snapshot instead of failing closed.
expected: Metrics counters, gauges, and observations should remain finite JSON numbers. Non-finite values should be rejected before a snapshot is written.
reproduction: Run a focused regression test that sets a gauge to NaN and asserts metrics snapshot creation rejects the value before writing JSON.
failing_test: Add a focused regression test under tests/unit that proves non-finite metrics values are rejected.
impact: Invalid metrics JSON can break nats-sink-metrics, observability policy exports, Prometheus textfile generation, service diagnostics, and release evidence pipelines.
delivery_semantics: No delivery semantics are changed. Metrics remain observational and must not affect commit-then-ACK behavior.
security: The fix must not include metric values from private payloads or logs. Error messages should name the metric field but not expose sensitive data.
acceptance: A focused regression test covers non-finite gauge rejection.
A focused regression test covers non-finite observation rejection.
load_metrics_snapshot() rejects non-standard constants in snapshot files.
nats-sink-metrics continues to read valid snapshots.
Existing unit, smoke, documentation, package, and release checks still pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, metrics tests, observability tests, metrics CLI tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md and metrics documentation to state that metric values must be finite JSON numbers.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published. Include the release tag, regression evidence, verification evidence, and any remaining limitations.
